keyMessages: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
Pathways programs help educators identify a challenge, choose a pathway, and continue learning through partnership.
WCEPS supports schools and districts with educational resources, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
proofPoints: WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
The site says WCEPS extends the impact of University of Wisconsin-Madison educational innovations.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a structured one-pager with a summary column, key message cards, evidence-backed proof points, and a bottom CTA band.

headlineOptions: Chart a pathway for your school or district
Customized educational solutions for education teams
subheadOptions: Explore WCEPS Pathways programs and services across resources, publications, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
WCEPS helps schools and districts review needs, compare options, and continue learning through ongoing partnership.
A 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit, WCEPS extends UW-Madison educational innovations into customized support for educators.
copy.body: WCEPS offers Pathways programs and services for schools and districts seeking research-based educational resources and customized educational solutions. Education leaders can compare options across publications, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses, then connect with WCEPS to discuss district challenges and goals.
bullets: Start with the challenge or need your education team wants to address.
Compare pathway options for resources, assessments, professional learning, evaluations, and alignment analyses.
Use a consultative process that listens to district needs and considers how programs can help teams reach their goals.
Support ongoing partnership and continuous learning for educators.
Work with a nonprofit organization focused on extending research-based educational innovations to schools and districts.

fittedCopy.headline: Chart a pathway for your school or district
fittedCopy.deck: Explore WCEPS Pathways programs and services across resources, publications, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content.
proofPoints: Start with the challenge or need your education team wants.
Compare pathway options for resources, assessments, professional learning, evaluations.
Use a consultative process that listens to district needs.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: magazine headline, one deck, short proof rail
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: edge-to-edge editorial art column or band
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: large type block with compact editorial proof band

designRecipe.hierarchy: magazine one-pager with dominant left headline zone, concise deck beneath, compact proof-band rhythm, and action emphasis at the lower text area
designRecipe.artDirection: premium education-leadership editorial concept for WCEPS Pathways: a confident right-field visual suggesting guided routes, district decision-making, program comparison, and evidence-informed support through refined maps, layered paper, subtle data cues, and warm institutional color
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: create natural copy-safe space through composition, contrast, and quiet negative areas; imply the proof-band cadence with visual pacing only, without fake text, logos, boxes, cards, or UI elements
designRecipe.appComposition: app places the official WCEPS logo, real headline, deck, proof points, CTA, spacing, and export; generated art supplies the full-page editorial structure and right-field visual energy
promptDirectives: Design a complete letter-portrait editorial one-pager concept, not a decorative background.
Keep the left side naturally calm for app-set text and the right field visually active with pathway-inspired education imagery.
Use sophisticated academic colors, layered depth, and precise cropping suitable for district leaders.
Do not render words, logos, CTA buttons, placeholder boxes, contact bars, or fake interface modules.

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a portrait full-bleed campaign art plate, 1024x1536, premium editorial marketing concept for a nonprofit education support brand. The art must be entirely text-free and logo-free, designed as a background/art plate that an app will later overlay with official logo, headline, deck, proof points, CTA, and source notes.

Composition: magazine one-pager hierarchy with a calm, naturally copy-safe left text zone occupying roughly the left 42–48% of the page, using quiet negative space, subtle depth, and low-detail tonal contrast without boxes or panels. The right field should carry the main visual energy: confident, warm institutional education-sector storytelling suggesting guided routes, pathways, district support, and collaborative planning. Maintain important visual energy away from trim edges and preserve a polished portrait crop.

Visual subject/style: authentic editorial photography feel or refined abstract-illustrative atmosphere, not stocky or posed. Show an inclusive district/education team planning support in a bright school or office setting, with educators reviewing materials, strategy packets, classroom support resources, and planning notes. Faces should not be close-up; hands should not be overly detailed. Convey research-informed professional learning, customized educational support, service, pathways, and leadership through mood, composition, and props only. Use layered depth: foreground soft-focus planning materials and subtle route-like lines or desk-edge perspective leading into the right-field team conversation; background bright but controlled.

Color palette: sophisticated academic colors anchored in deep navy #142836, teal #0081A4, aqua #3EB3BD, and green #5EA974, with warm neutral daylight. Use rich visual density on the right and quieter contrast on the left. Avoid washed-out empty fades, generic meeting-table cliches, graduation imagery, fake university marks, literal logo-shape wallpaper, chevrons, arrows, mountains, badges, seals, icons, charts, UI, cards, contact bars, placeholder boxes, or document layouts.

Strict text rule: absolutely no readable text, letters, numbers, labels, fake paragraphs, book titles, publisher names, email addresses, URLs, phone numbers, citations, lorem ipsum, signage, whiteboard writing, or any logo-like marks. Do not render WCEPS logo, initials, wordmark, or any invented brand mark. No CTA buttons, headlines, labels, proof copy, source evidence, or app layout scaffolding.

Quality: NY media/ad-agency quality, polished design-comp art plate, intentional crop, credible human nonprofit education atmosphere, finished visual hierarchy, layered depth, premium institutional overview, useful editorial density, no template scaffolding.

request.topic: research-based educational supports and service pathways
request.notes: Use the parent brand without over-describing individual programs.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Discover your pathway through WCEPS’ research-based educational resources, publications, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
sourceEvidence.label: Homepage positioning

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Not sure which pathway works best for your school? Reach out to one of our experts to guide you on a pathway to success. Compare Programs.
sourceEvidence.label: Pathway comparison CTA

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Our Pathways offerings encompass a variety of resources educators all over the world need to be successful in the classroom.
sourceEvidence.label: About Pathways offerings

sourceEvidence.excerpt: We strive to listen to what our district colleagues need, understand your challenges, and consider how our programs can help you reach your goals.
sourceEvidence.label: Consultative tone
